rationale_for_baseline_tier: Active upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage — coffee-ground emesis plus melaena — with haemodynamic compromise (orthostatic hypotension 95/60 to 80/50, tachycardia HR ~115, presyncope on standing, pallor and diaphoresis). This is an immediate threat to life from ongoing blood loss; the only appropriate tier is T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) for resuscitation and urgent endoscopic haemostasis. The time-critical features (coffee-ground vomit, black tarry stool, near-collapse on standing) are directly PATIENT/CAREGIVER-reportable, which is what warrants a genuine T5 uplift rather than a T3 in-person-exam-compensation ceiling. Endoscopy is also required to exclude a malignant ulcer given the weight-loss alarm feature; variceal bleed and perforation cannot be excluded remotely and share the same emergency pathway.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Vomiting fresh red blood, or ongoing/worsening coffee-ground vomiting or melaena before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — keep 000 on the line and ask the dispatcher to upgrade priority
escalation_edges.rationale: Fresh haematemesis indicates brisk ongoing bleeding and imminent decompensation; paramedic resuscitation must be expedited.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he vomits fresh red blood or brings up more of the dark grainy material, tell 000 straight away so they can upgrade the ambulance to top priority.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Collapse, loss of consciousness, or becoming unresponsive
escalation_edges.rationale: Collapse signals decompensated haemorrhagic shock; lay flat with legs raised, keep warm, and be ready to start CPR if breathing stops.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he passes out, lay him flat and raise his legs, keep him warm, and tell 000 immediately — start CPR if he stops breathing and you know how.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Untreated Helicobacter pylori and an upper GI alarm feature (unintentional weight loss, early satiety)
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure the emergency handover flags the need for urgent upper GI endoscopy with biopsy to exclude malignancy and for H. pylori eradication once the acute bleed is controlled — this must not be lost in the acute resuscitation.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Spouse is present and made the call
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Direct the spouse to unlock the door for paramedics, stay with the patient, keep him flat and nil by mouth, and relay any deterioration to the 000 dispatcher.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now and tell them he is vomiting blood (coffee-ground material), has passed black tarry stool and is faint on standing. Keep him lying flat — do not let him sit up, stand or walk. Give him nothing to eat or drink. Unlock the front door for the paramedics and stay with him. If he vomits fresh red blood or passes out, tell 000 immediately.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or a GP appointment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary after endoscopy; the GP should ensure Helicobacter pylori eradication is completed and confirmed, and that endoscopic/biopsy follow-up for malignancy exclusion is arranged.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating an active upper GI bleed with haemodynamic compromise as indigestion/gastritis or a GP/urgent-care problem instead of an emergency.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Continued haemorrhage to decompensated hypovolaemic shock and death while the patient waits at home or for a routine appointment.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The specific bleeding source (peptic ulcer vs variceal vs malignant ulcer) and the exact volume of blood loss cannot be determined via telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Act on the recognised emergency — an active upper GI bleed with haemodynamic compromise — and escalate to 000; the emergency pathway is identical regardless of the exact source.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Delaying escalation to gather more history, or reassuring the patient that a benign cause is confirmed and home management is safe.
